title: Melicope saint-johnii
intro: Melicope saint-johnii, also known as Saint John's Melicope or Saint John's Wood's Weed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as the potential to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. The bioactive constituents of Melicope saint-johnii include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and promote relaxation.
